title: Mustardy Canadian Pork Braise
description: A rich, savory pork braise featuring a tangy mustard sauce, crispy Canadian bacon, and a drizzle of sesame sauce.
instructions: Season the pork cubes with salt, black pepper, and caraway seeds.
Heat a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Sear the pork cubes until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the pot and set aside. Reduce heat to medium and cook the sliced onion until caramelized, about 10 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the pot and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant.
Add the browned pork back into the pot, along with the Dijon mustard and balsamic vinegar. Stir to combine.
Cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven. Braise the pork for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until tender and easily shredded.
Remove the pot from the oven and stir in the sesame sauce. Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the pork with crispy Canadian bacon on top, garnished with fresh parsley or thyme if desired.
Drizzle the remaining sesame sauce over the top of the dish, if desired.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
